name: 大理
description: Dali is a picturesque town in Yunnan province, set against the dramatic backdrop of Cangshan Mountains and beside the crystal-clear Erhai Lake. Home to the Bai ethnic minority with 1,000 years of history. Known for cobblestone streets, traditional Bai architecture, and vibrant local culture. A laid-back atmosphere perfect for cycling, hiking, and cultural immersion.
highlights: Erhai Lake - Scenic cycling and views
Three Pagodas of Dali - Buddhist temples
Dali Old Town - Traditional Bai architecture

climate.tips: Spring (March-May) is flower season with mild weather. Autumn (Sept-Oct) has clear skies and comfortable temps. Summer is rainy but green. Winter is dry and sunny but cold. Bring layers - temperature swings daily.

attractions.name: 洱海
attractions.description: A beautiful highland lake often called 'Sea of Clouds' offering scenic cycling paths, traditional fishing villages, and stunning mountain backdrops.
attractions.address: Erhai Lake, Dali Bai Autonomous Prefecture
attractions.tips: Rent a bicycle (40-80¥/day) to circle the lake. Start early to avoid tour buses. Best light for photos in early morning.

attractions.name: 大理三塔
attractions.description: Iconic trio of ancient Buddhist pagodas dating back to the 9th century, symbol of Dali's historic Nanzhao Kingdom.
attractions.tips: Visit in morning to avoid tour crowds. Combine with nearby Butterfly Spring in spring. Good photo opportunities from temple courtyard.

attractions.name: 大理古城
attractions.description: A beautifully preserved ancient town with traditional Bai architecture, cobblestone streets, and lively local culture.
attractions.tips: Stay overnight to experience night atmosphere. Early morning before shops open for photos. Explore side alleys for authentic Bai life.

attractions.name: 喜洲古镇
attractions.description: Well-preserved Bai ethnic town famous for its architecture, local markets, and authentic Bai way of life.
attractions.tips: Visit on market day (Friday, Saturday) for authentic experience. Try Baba (Bai flatbread) from vendors. Zhou's compound is worth entrance fee.

attractions.name: 双廊
attractions.description: A serene lakeside village on Erhai Lake's eastern shore, known for scenic beauty, boutique hotels, and photography spots.
attractions.tips: Stay overnight at boutique lakeside hotels. Best sunset views from Nantian. Rent electric bike to explore coastline.

attractions.name: 苍山
attractions.description: A dramatic mountain range behind Dali with hiking trails, ancient temples, and panoramic views of Erhai Lake.
attractions.tips: Take the cable car up for easier hiking. Weather changes quickly - bring layers and rain gear. Hiking trail is well marked but steep.

attractions.name: 巍宝山
attractions.description: A sacred Taoist mountain with ancient temples, hiking trails, and panoramic views of the Dali valley.
attractions.tips: Less visited than Cangshan. Good for hikers seeking solitude. Bring water and snacks. Start in morning.

attractions.name: 蝴蝶泉
attractions.description: A legendary spring surrounded by ancient trees, traditionally associated with butterfly mating season in spring.
attractions.address: Butterfly Spring Park, 20km north of Dali
attractions.tips: Best in April-May when actual butterflies appear. Combine with Xizhou and Erhai Lake cycling. Performance times: 9:30, 11:00, 14:00, 15:30.

attractions.name: 周城扎染
attractions.description: The largest Bai ethnic village famous for traditional tie-dye crafts. Over 90% of residents are involved in tie-dye production.
attractions.tips: Visit morning market on weekends. Try DIY tie-dye experience. Best light for photos in early morning.

attractions.name: 巍山古城
attractions.description: A perfectly preserved ancient city and the birthplace of the Nanzhao Kingdom. Less touristy than Dali Old Town with authentic local life.
attractions.tips: Come for the torch festival (July-August). Try Weishan's famous Nanjiang rice noodles. Start from the south gate.

attractions.name: 南诏风情岛
attractions.description: A small island in Erhai Lake showcasing Nanzhao Kingdom culture with statues, temples, and traditional Bai architecture.
attractions.tips: Combine with Shuanglang visit. Take the boat to the island. Best visited in morning.

attractions.name: 小普陀
attractions.description: A tiny island temple in Erhai Lake with a small pagoda, one of the most photographed spots in Dali.
attractions.tips: Best at sunrise for photography. Can feed seagulls from the boat. Combine with cycling the eastern shore.

attractions.name: 洗马潭
attractions.description: A high-altitude alpine meadow and marsh at Cangshan Mountain, accessible by cable car with stunning views.
attractions.tips: Take the cable car up. Weather changes rapidly - bring warm layers. Best visited in summer for wildflowers.

attractions.name: 天龙洞
attractions.description: Ancient Buddhist cave temple with colorful statues and carvings dating back to the Nanzhao Kingdom period.
attractions.tips: Combine with Cangshan hike. The cave is smaller than it looks from outside. Good for shelter if raining.

attractions.name: 洱源西湖
attractions.description: A beautiful lake area with wetland scenery, lotus fields, and traditional Bai fishing villages. Less crowded than Erhai Lake.
attractions.tips: Best in summer for lotus. Take a boat tour through the waterways. Good for cycling combined with nearby temples.

attractions.name: 喜洲海舌生态公园
attractions.description: A peninsula extending into Erhai Lake, offering 180-degree views of the lake and Cangshan Mountains.
attractions.tips: Best at sunrise or sunset. Very crowded on weekends. Combine with Xizhou Baba tour.

attractions.name: 大理州博物馆
attractions.description: A modern museum showcasing Dali's history from Nanzhao Kingdom to present, with artifacts and interactive exhibits.
attractions.tips: Good for rainy days. English descriptions available. Combine with nearby Erhai Lake walk.

attractions.name: 寂照庵
attractions.description: A serene Buddhist temple famous for its thousands of potted flowers and peaceful atmosphere.
attractions.tips: Try the vegetarian food. Very peaceful. Wear comfortable shoes for the walk up. Bring cash for donations.

attractions.name: 张家花园
attractions.description: A restored Bai courtyard garden showcasing traditional Bai architecture, with tea tasting and cultural performances.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online for discounts. Combine tea tasting and performance. Good for learning about Bai culture.

attractions.name: 大理学院情人坝
attractions.description: A scenic embankment near Dali University popular with students and couples, offering beautiful Erhai Lake views.
attractions.tips: Best at sunset. Nearby small restaurants and cafes. Popular with local students.

attractions.name: 鹿卧山
attractions.description: Hillside viewpoint between Dali Old Town and Erhai Lake, offering scenic views of both.
attractions.address: Between Dali Old Town and Erhai
attractions.tips: Best at sunset for photos with both town and lake. Can be crowded on weekends.

attractions.name: 乌龙坝草甸
attractions.description: Highland grassland area with windmills, offering scenic views and photography opportunities.
attractions.tips: Less visited, great for escaping crowds. Bring food and water. Best in spring and summer.

restaurants.name: 三塔寺素斋
restaurants.description: Simple vegetarian restaurant near the pagodas serving Buddhist-style meat substitutes and local vegetables.

restaurants.name: 古城第一凉鸡米线
restaurants.nameEn: Dali First Cold Chicken Rice Noodles
restaurants.description: Legendary local spot famous for cold chicken rice noodles - a Dali specialty with tangy sauce and fresh ingredients.

restaurants.name: 苍山道素食
restaurants.address: Yeyou Road, near Dali Old Town
restaurants.description: Cozy vegetarian restaurant with organic ingredients, creative dishes, and peaceful atmosphere near the old town.

restaurants.name: 双廊海韵
restaurants.description: Lakeside restaurant with Erhai views, serving fresh fish from the lake and Yunnan specialties.

restaurants.name: 永祥火把腿
restaurants.description: Famous for Yunnan ham and leg meat, a local specialty snack perfect for quick lunch or snack.

restaurants.name: 大理古城咖啡馆
restaurants.description: Lively cafe in the old town serving Yunnan coffee, Western breakfasts, and fusion dishes popular with expats.

restaurants.name: 喜洲破酥粑粑
restaurants.description: Famous street food - crispy Baba flatbread with sweet or savory fillings, a Bai specialty.

restaurants.name: 花与餐厅
restaurants.description: Romantic garden restaurant with Erhai views, serving creative Yunnan cuisine with fresh local ingredients.

restaurants.name: 白族菜馆
restaurants.description: Authentic Bai cuisine restaurant serving traditional dishes like Bender (Bai cold chicken), Rushan cheese, and Sai ao (rice noodle rolls).

restaurants.name: 饵丝飘香
restaurants.description: Famous local spot for Ersi (rice noodle strips) in savory soup, a Dali breakfast staple.

restaurants.name: 酸菜鱼庄
restaurants.description: Popular restaurant specializing in sour cabbage fish hotpot made with fresh Erhai Lake fish and homemade pickled cabbage.

restaurants.name: 乳扇烧烤摊
restaurants.description: Famous street food stall serving grilled Rushan (Bai milk cheese) skewers, a must-try Dali snack.

restaurants.name: 鲜花饼总店
restaurants.description: Famous bakery specializing in flower cakes - delicate pastries filled with fresh rose and other edible flowers.

restaurants.name: 米凉虾小馆
restaurants.description: Local dessert shop serving Mi Liangxia - shrimp-shaped rice jelly in sweet osmanthus syrup, a Dali specialty.

restaurants.name: 野生菌火锅
restaurants.description: Premium hotpot restaurant featuring fresh wild mushrooms from Yunnan mountains, served in nourishing broth.

restaurants.name: 洱海鱼村
restaurants.description: Riverside restaurant in fishing village serving fresh Erhai Lake fish prepared in various styles.

restaurants.name: 白族三道茶茶馆
restaurants.description: Traditional tea house offering authentic Bai Three Tea Ceremony - bitter tea, sweet tea, and aftertaste tea.

restaurants.name: 青梅酒铺
restaurants.description: Cozy bar specializing in local plum wine and fruit wines made from Yunnan's fresh fruits.

restaurants.name: 当地烧烤一条街
restaurants.address: Dali Old Town, south gate area
restaurants.description: Famous BBQ street with multiple stalls serving Yunnan-style grilled meat, mushrooms, and vegetables.

restaurants.name: 巍山一根面
restaurants.description: Legendary noodle shop famous for One Strand Noodles - a single kilometer-long noodle served in one bowl.

restaurants.name: 白族特色凉拌菜
restaurants.description: Local specialty shop at Xizhou market serving various Bai-style cold salads with fresh vegetables and herbs.

restaurants.name: 烤全羊
restaurants.description: Traditional whole lamb roasting experience in scenic countryside setting, perfect for groups.

restaurants.name: 银桥米线馆
restaurants.description: Local spot famous for rice noodles in rich broth, popular with locals and in-the-know travelers.

restaurants.name: 双廊咖啡馆
restaurants.description: Cozy lakeside cafe with Erhai views, serving Yunnan coffee, Western breakfasts, and homemade pastries.

restaurants.name: 大理壹号院
restaurants.description: Upscale Bai cuisine restaurant in a traditional courtyard, offering refined local specialties with elegant presentation.

restaurants.name: 小邑庄白族菜
restaurants.address: Xiaoyi Village, near Dali Old Town
restaurants.description: Local family-run restaurant serving authentic Bai home-style dishes in a traditional courtyard.

restaurants.name: 巍山老街面馆
restaurants.description: Legendary single-noodle shop famous for one long strand noodle in rich broth, a must-try in Weishan.

restaurants.name: 才村湖景餐厅
restaurants.description: Lakeside restaurant with Erhai views, serving fresh fish from the lake and Yunnan specialties.

restaurants.name: 桃溪牧场
restaurants.description: Countryside restaurant specializing in grilled meats and fresh vegetables in scenic mountain setting.

restaurants.name: 床山咖啡
restaurants.description: Specialty coffee shop serving Yunnan beans, pour-overs, and espresso drinks in cozy atmosphere.

restaurants.name: 喜洲甜品店
restaurants.description: Local sweet shop famous for rice-based desserts, sweet rice balls, and traditional Bai snacks.

restaurants.name: 下关沱茶馆
restaurants.description: Traditional tea house specializing in Yunnan Pu'er tea with tea tasting and purchase options.

arrival.frequency: Daily flights from Kunming, some seasonal
arrival.tips: Airport 13km from Dali Old Town. Airport bus or taxi to town. Flights can be delayed in fog.

arrival.frequency: Daily trains from major Yunnan's cities
arrival.tips: New high-speed train from Kunming is scenic but slow. Old train is faster. Dali Station is closer to old town than new.
Bus from Kunming's Xiying Hotel is comfortable. Dali Bus Station is near old town.

bus: Local buses connect Dali to Xizhou, Shuanglang
Bus 4 to Xizhou from Dali Old Town
Bus to Shuanglang from Dali (2 hours)
Song of the River bus for scenic route
bicycle: Rent bicycles near Dali Old Town: 30-80¥/day
electric-bike: Popular way to explore Erhai Lake area
Need to recharge for long trips
metro: No metro system in Dali old town
bike: Bike rental shops in Dali old town
Electric bikes recommended for Erhai cycling

hotels.name: 大理古城海景酒店
hotels.nameEn: Dali Old Town Lake View Hotel
hotels.bookingTips: Book well ahead in peak season. Request lake view room.

hotels.name: 双廊海角酒店
hotels.bookingTips: The best lakeside area in Dali. Premium for Nantian views.

hotels.name: 大理中和坊酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good value in old town. Book inner courtyard rooms for quiet.

hotels.name: 才村码头客栈
hotels.bookingTips: Great for cyclists. Families run many guesthouses here.

hotels.name: 大理国际大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for comfort seekers. Good location between old town and lake.

hotels.name: 大理古城兰陵阁
hotels.bookingTips: Book inner rooms for quiet. Good value for location.

hotels.name: 双廊湖思茶屋
hotels.bookingTips: Premium lakeside location. Book well ahead in peak season.

hotels.name: 才村民宿
hotels.bookingTips: Great for cyclists. Locals are very friendly.

hotels.name: 大理风花雪月酒店
hotels.nameEn: Dali Wind Flower Snow Moon Hotel
hotels.bookingTips: Good balance of comfort and location.

hotels.name: 喜洲杨家拼图
hotels.bookingTips: Experience traditional Bai living. Book during market day.

hotels.name: 大理背包客青年旅舍
hotels.bookingTips: Great for solo travelers. Evening pub crawls organized.

hotels.name: 双廊晴天见
hotels.bookingTips: Best sunrise location in Shuanglang. Book months ahead.

hotels.name: 挖色洱海客栈
hotels.bookingTips: Good alternative to Shuanglang. Very peaceful.

hotels.name: 大理苍山饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Good base for exploring. Request mountain view rooms.

hotels.name: 三塔寺周边民宿
hotels.bookingTips: Good for temple visitors. Very peaceful mornings.

hotels.name: 银桥花语客栈
hotels.bookingTips: Great for photographers. Perfect for flower season.

hotels.name: 周城白族客栈
hotels.bookingTips: Best for culture seekers. Morning market is walking distance.

hotels.name: 巍山古城客栈
hotels.bookingTips: Perfect for escaping tourism. Nanzhao Kingdom atmosphere.

tips: May not work in remote villages

payment.description: Essential for markets and remote areas

culturalTips.title: Bai Culture
culturalTips.content: Dali is home to the Bai ethnic minority. Respect local customs. The Bai have unique traditions including the 'Three-Angel' dress and tie-dye crafts. March Fair and绕三灵 festival are major events.

culturalTips.title: Erhai Cycling
culturalTips.content: Cycling Erhai Lake is the signature experience. 120km around the lake. Start early, bring water and sunscreen. Rent bikes in Dali or Caicun. The eastern shore is less crowded.

culturalTips.title: Mountain Weather
culturalTips.content: Dali's weather changes quickly. Mountains cause localized weather systems. Even in summer, bring a jacket. Rain can appear suddenly. Spring flowers and autumn colors are spectacular.

culturalTips.title: Temple Etiquette
culturalTips.content: When visiting Three Pagodas and temples, dress modestly covering shoulders and knees. Remove hats in temples. Don't point feet at Buddha images. Walk clockwise around pagodas.

culturalTips.title: Bai Tie-dye Culture
culturalTips.content: The Bai are famous for traditional tie-dye (扎染), using natural plant dyes on cotton fabric. Each pattern has symbolic meaning. Zhoucheng village is the center of this craft. Look for butterfly, fish, and flower patterns.

culturalTips.title: Yunnan Tea Culture
culturalTips.content: Yunnan is famous for Pu'er tea. Dali has tea houses where you can experience tea ceremony. The Bai Three Tea Ceremony (三道茶) represents life flavors: bitter, sweet, and aftertaste. Tea is an important part of local social life.

culturalTips.title: Bai Festivals
culturalTips.content: Major Bai festivals include: March Fair (三月街) - horse trading and trading fair in Dali; Rao San Ling (绕三灵) - pilgrimage to Three Sacred Places; Torch Festival (火把节) - lighting torches to ward off pests. Summer and early autumn are festival seasons.

culturalTips.title: Bai Architecture
culturalTips.content: Bai houses feature white walls, grey tiles, and horse-head roofs. Three-in-one courtyards (三坊一照壁) are typical. Look for the精美的门楼 (ornate gate towers) and paintings on walls inside courtyards.

culturalTips.title: Bai Traditional Clothing
culturalTips.content: Bai women wear 'Three-Angel' costumes (三道茶) with white jackets, blue belts, and embroidered shoes. The famous 'Phoenix Crown' headdress symbolizes the phoenix. You can rent costumes for photos in tourist areas.

culturalTips.title: Bai Religion
culturalTips.content: The Bai follow a mix of Buddhism, Taoism, and Benzhu (本主) worship - worship of local deities. Every village has a Benzhu temple. The Three Pagodas are important Buddhist sites. Be respectful when observing rituals.

culturalTips.title: Bai Music
culturalTips.content: Bai opera (白剧) combines singing, dancing, and acting. Traditional instruments include the Sanxian (三弦) and Horse-head fiddle. You can catch performances at Dali Ancient Town or during festivals.

culturalTips.title: Bai Food Culture
culturalTips.content: Bai cuisine features fresh vegetables, mushrooms, and fish. Rushan (乳扇) is stretched milk cheese. Bender (凉鸡) is cold chicken. Try local specialties at markets: Baba (粑粑) flatbread, Ersi (饵丝) rice noodles, and Mi Liangxia (米凉虾) shrimp-shaped jelly dessert.

culturalTips.title: Bai Silver Crafts
culturalTips.content: Bai silver smithing is famous for intricate jewelry and decorative items. Silver is believed to have health benefits. Popular items include silver bracelets, hair ornaments, and tea accessories. Authentic silver has high purity.

culturalTips.title: Bai Wedding Customs
culturalTips.content: Bai weddings feature unique customs including the 'Jiao Zhai' (咬嫁) ritual where the bride bites the groom's ear. Wedding feasts are elaborate multi-course affairs. The bride changes into multiple costumes during the ceremony.

culturalTips.title: Erhai Fishing Culture
culturalTips.content: Fishing has been central to Erhai life for centuries. Traditional fishing methods include using cormorants (鸬鹚). Some fishing villages still practice these methods. Fish dishes are staples of local cuisine.

culturalTips.title: Bai Language
culturalTips.content: The Bai language (白语) is tonal and completely different from Mandarin. Many locals speak both Bai and Mandarin. Learning a few Bai phrases like 'Ni hao' (hello) and ' Xie xie' (thank you) in Bai will be appreciated.

culturalTips.title: Photography Tips
culturalTips.content: Dali offers excellent photography opportunities: sunrise at Erhai Lake, the pink wall in Xizhou, Cangshan Mountain reflections, and Bai people in traditional clothing. The best light is early morning and late afternoon. Bring a tripod for night shots of the Three Pagodas.

culturalTips.title: Dali Shopping
culturalTips.content: Dali is famous for tie-dye products, silver jewelry, tea (especially Pu'er), and marble. The best shopping is at the morning market in Xizhou and Zhoucheng for tie-dye, and the Foreigners Street in Dali Old Town for silver and souvenirs. Bargaining is expected in markets.

culturalTips.title: Altitude and Health
culturalTips.content: Dali sits at about 1972m elevation. Some visitors may experience mild altitude sickness. Stay hydrated, avoid strenuous activity on arrival, and eat lightly. The medical facilities in Dali are adequate for basic health issues.

culturalTips.title: English in Dali
culturalTips.content: English is limited in Dali outside tourist areas. Learning a few Mandarin phrases helps. Many young people in Dali Old Town and Shuanglang speak basic English. Translation apps are helpful for communicating with older locals.

culturalTips.title: Environmental Awareness
culturalTips.content: Erhai Lake's ecosystem is fragile. Avoid single-use plastics, don't swim in restricted areas, and use eco-friendly transportation. Several initiatives promote lake protection. Be a responsible tourist.

culturalTips.title: Nanzhao Kingdom Heritage
culturalTips.content: Dali was the capital of the Nanzhao Kingdom (738-902 AD), one of the longest-ruling dynasties in Chinese history. The Three Pagodas, Nanzhao Island, and Weishan Ancient Town reflect this heritage. Understanding this history enriches the visit.

culturalTips.title: Yunnan Coffee Culture
culturalTips.content: Yunnan produces some of China's best coffee. Dali has several specialty coffee shops using locally grown beans. Try the Yunnan pour-over at cafes on Foreigners Street. Coffee plantations are nearby and offer tours.

culturalTips.title: LGBTQ+ Friendly Destination
culturalTips.content: Dali has a reputation as one of China's more LGBTQ+ friendly destinations, with a relatively open atmosphere and welcoming vibe. Several bars and cafes cater to the community. The laid-back atmosphere is generally accepting.

culturalTips.title: Digital Nomad Hub
culturalTips.content: Dali has become a popular base for digital nomads and remote workers. Many cafes offer fast WiFi and quiet workspaces. Monthly apartment rentals are affordable. Coworking spaces are available in Dali Old Town.

culturalTips.title: Dali Nightlife
culturalTips.content: Dali has a vibrant nightlife scene around Foreigners Street with bars, live music, and clubs. Shuanglang has a more relaxed evening atmosphere with lake-view bars. Most nightlife closes by midnight on weekdays.

culturalTips.title: Best Time to Visit
culturalTips.content: The best times to visit Dali are March-May (spring flower season) and September-November (clear skies, autumn colors). Summer (June-August) is rainy but green. Winter is dry and sunny but cold. Avoid Chinese holidays when tourist sites are crowded.
